raw_responses.query: stigmergy ratchet LLM multi-agent coordination 2025 arxiv

results.title: CodeCRDT: Stigmergic coordination in autonomous LLM agents
results.snippet: CodeCRDT applies stigmergy to autonomous LLM agents, where agents coordinate through monitoring shared state rather than explicit message passing, observing edits, skipping completed work, and avoiding conflicts without centralized task assignment. Includes a formal TODO-claim protocol with provable safety under strong eventual consistency.

results.title: Emergent Collective Memory in Decentralized Multi-Agent AI Systems
results.snippet: Stigmergy, wherein agents communicate indirectly through modifications of the environment, enables scalable coordination without centralized control. Stigmergic coordination becomes superior at high densities because overlapping trajectories create reliable environmental signals.

results.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration Mechanisms: A Survey of LLMs
results.snippet: A comprehensive framework characterizes collaboration mechanisms based on dimensions including actors, types, structures, strategies, and coordination protocols.

results.title: Ledger-State Stigmergy: A Formal Framework for Indirect Coordination Grounded in Distributed Ledger State
results.snippet: The ledger functions as a replicated shared-state medium through which decentralized agents coordinate indirectly, mirroring stigmergy where organisms coordinate through traces left in a shared environment, with mature formalizations in swarm intelligence and multi-agent systems.

results.title: Coordination as an Architectural Layer for LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
results.snippet: Information-controlled empirical study on prediction markets in multi-agent LLM coordination.

results.title: Reinforcement Learning for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems through Orchestration Traces
results.snippet: Orchestration traces in LLM multi-agent systems with reinforcement learning.

results.title: A-Mem: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents
results.snippet: System maintains LLMs' capability to retain long-term memory through a memory stream and controller mechanism.

results.title: Detailed balance in large language model-driven agents
results.snippet: Examines thermodynamic detailed balance properties in LLM-driven agent systems.

results.title: Externalization in LLM Agents: A Unified Review of Memory, Skills, Protocols and Harness Engineering
results.snippet: Survey of externalization mechanisms in LLM agent design including append-only persistent memory.

results.title: Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Emerging Frontiers
results.snippet: Six core memory operations including Consolidation, Updating, Indexing, Forgetting, Retrieval, Condensation.

results.title: Rethinking Memory in LLM based Agents: Representations, Operations, and Emerging Topics
results.snippet: Taxonomy categorizes memory into parametric and contextual forms.
